task_completion.description: Navigate to the SMTP settings in the Rocket.Chat Administration panel
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rocket.Chat Administration panel is visible in a web browser, specifically showing the 'Email' settings page with the 'SMTP' section expanded or visible.
The 'Protocol' input field in the SMTP settings contains the exact text 'smtp'.
The 'Host' input field in the SMTP settings contains the exact text 'smtp.internal.corp'.
The 'Port' input field in the SMTP settings contains the exact text '2525'.
The 'IgnoreTLS' toggle or checkbox in the SMTP settings is set to the enabled/on position.

task_completion.description: Leave Username and Password fields empty
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Username' and 'Password' input fields in the SMTP settings are completely empty.
A 'Save changes' button is clicked, appears disabled (indicating no unsaved changes), or a success notification (e.g., 'Settings saved') is visible on the screen.

integrity.description: The agent used the Rocket.Chat web interface to configure the settings
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a web browser with the Rocket.Chat UI being interacted with, rather than a terminal window executing curl commands or scripts to change settings.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine Rocket.Chat application
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the actual Rocket.Chat web interface, not a text editor with configuration files or a mock application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected SMTP settings are: Protocol: 'smtp', Host: 'smtp.internal.corp', Port: '2525', IgnoreTLS: Enabled (true). Username and Password must be left empty.
